nativeRange.habitat: Shallow tropical reef slopes, reef flats, and lagoons; commonly found plating outward on exposed substrates in moderate light and flow; tends to dominate flat-substrate shallow reef zones

decorPreferences: stable rockwork base for encrusting or plating growth
lower to mid rockwork placement with indirect or moderate light
ample open space around the colony (8–12 cm clearance from neighbors)
moderate indirect flow, avoid direct laminar blast on tissue

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae. Supplemental target feeding once or twice weekly with small meaty foods such as mysis shrimp, Oyster-Feast, Roti-Feast, cyclopeeze, or baby brine shrimp is generally beneficial for growth and coloration. Feeding tends to occur at night when sweeper tentacles are extended; a turkey baster or pipette can be used for targeted delivery.

goodWith: non-aggressive LPS corals placed well out of sweeper reach
avoidWith: corals within sweeper tentacle range (8–10 cm)
other Echinopora colonies without adequate spacing
aggressive LPS such as Galaxea or Hammer placed nearby
high-flow powerheads directed at colony surface

summary: Echinopora is a plating/encrusting LPS genus in family Merulinidae, commonly sold as 'Hedgehog Coral' or 'Blue Chalice Echinopora.' Generally intermediate care; prefers moderate light and flow with ample spacing due to sweeper tentacles.
careNotes:
Chalice-umbrella note: In the aquarium trade, 'Chalice Coral' is a broad commercial umbrella name applied to several genera — primarily Echinophyllia (see [echinophyllia-coral]) and Oxypora (see [oxypora-coral]), both in family Lobophylliidae — the true chalice clade — as well as Mycedium (see [mycedium-coral], family Merulinidae per WoRMS / Budd et al. 2012 — convergent plating morphology), Echinopora (this entry, also Merulinidae), Pectinia, and Lithophyllon. Both Echinopora and Mycedium belong to Merulinidae rather than Lobophylliidae, having remained in the old Faviidae aggregate rather than being reclassified. Despite the shared trade name, Echinopora is not closely related to Echinophyllia — the resemblance is convergent (both plate/encruste on hard substrate). ORA has produced aquacultured Echinopora strains including the well-known 'Sprung's Stunner' (E. lamellosa, purple base with bright green polyps) and other aquacultured color morphs sold under trade names like 'Firefly Echinopora' and 'Blue Chalice Echinopora.'

Taxonomy: Echinopora Lamarck, 1816 currently sits in family Merulinidae, order Scleractinia. Older sources may still list the family as Faviidae (pre-2012 taxonomy); Merulinidae is the accepted placement per WoRMS and current molecular systematics. The genus comprises approximately 13 recognized species, including E. lamellosa (the most common in trade and science), E. gemmacea, E. horrida, E. fruticulosa, and E. mammiformis. Most aquarium entries are sold at the genus level ('Echinopora sp.') or by color-morph trade name without species identification. Common names include Hedgehog Coral, Honeycomb Coral, and Spiny Plate Coral.

Growth form: Echinopora typically grow as thin laminar plates, encrusting sheets, or whorled tiered structures. In the wild, stands may exceed 5 meters across (Corals of the World); in captivity, colonies typically reach 10–40 cm. Growth is generally considered fast relative to other LPS, with encrusting expansion readily visible under good conditions.

Lighting: Cross-source averages from four aquarium sources (Reeflings, Vivid Aquariums, ReefChasers, Top Shelf Aquatics) converge on 75–200 PAR as a practical range, with 100–150 PAR as a common sweet spot. Echinopora tends to adapt to a wider light range than Echinophyllia or Oxypora, but very high PAR (250+) can cause bleaching. New specimens should be acclimated gradually over 2–3 weeks starting at lower light.

Flow: Moderate flow is preferred — sufficient to oxygenate tissue and clear detritus, but not so strong as to cause polyp retraction. Sources agree that direct laminar flow should be avoided; indirect chaotic flow is better.

Aggression: Echinopora extend sweeper tentacles at night — typically reported at 5–10 cm in length (Reeflex: up to 10 cm; task brief: 5–10 cm). Placement must account for full nighttime extension. Maintain at least 8–12 cm clearance in all directions.

Water quality: Stable parameters are important. Nitrates around 2–10 ppm and phosphates 0.03–0.1 ppm are reported as workable; extremely low-nutrient systems may cause color loss. Calcium, alkalinity, and magnesium supplementation as for other LPS is recommended. Cross-source parameter averages: temperature 23–27°C (Reeflings 24–27°C; TankFacts 23.3–28.3°C; Quality Marine 22.2–26.7°C); salinity 1.023–1.026; kH 8–10 dKH; calcium 400–450 ppm; magnesium 1250–1350 ppm.

Care level: Sources range from 'easy to moderate' (Reeflings, E. lamellosa specifically) to 'intermediate' (ReefChasers, Quality Marine) to 'moderate to difficult' (Reeflings, E. horrida specifically). The genus-level designation of 'intermediate' reflects the spectrum — E. lamellosa and most ORA aquacultured strains are among the more forgiving LPS, while wild-collected E. horrida can be more sensitive.

Fragging: Relatively straightforward — colonies can be fragged with bone cutters or a tile saw. Frags of 2.5 cm or larger encrust and recover well on frag plugs or rubble with coral glue. Dipping new frags before introduction is advisable.
breedingNotes: Fragging is the primary propagation method in captivity. ORA has successfully aquacultured multiple Echinopora strains, making captive-bred frags widely available through LFS and online coral vendors. Sexual broadcast spawning occurs on wild reefs but is not practiced in home aquaria.

primary.name: Vivid Aquariums – Firefly Echinopora Coral
primary.notes: Primary source for husbandry parameters: PAR 80–250, temperature 75–80°F, salinity 1.024–1.026, pH 8.1–8.4, alkalinity 8–9.5 dKH, calcium 420–440 ppm, magnesium 1260–1350 ppm, feeding recommendations. Used as primary after WWC (404) and BRS care article (404) both failed.

additional.name: Reeflings – Hedgehog Coral (Echinopora lamellosa) Care Guide
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (24–27°C), salinity (1.023–1.025), pH (8.1–8.4), lighting (150–250 PAR), flow (moderate), aggression, and care level. Also confirmed common names (Hedgehog Coral, Honeycomb Coral, Spiny Plate Coral, Blue Chalice). Minor disagreement on PAR upper bound (250 vs 200) resolved by averaging across all sources.

additional.name: Reeflex – Echinopora lamellosa
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (24.7–28.9°C), minimum tank size (150L/33 gal), adult captive size (up to 25 cm), native range (Indo-Pacific, depth 3–35 m), sweeper tentacle length (up to 10 cm), flow (moderate), feeding (phyto/zooplankton). Family listed as Merulinidae, order Scleractinia confirmed.

additional.name: ReefChasers – Chalice Coral Care Guide
additional.notes: Confirmed care level (intermediate), flow (moderate indirect), PAR range (75–100 for chalice genus), alkalinity (8–9 dKH), calcium (430–460 ppm). Confirmed Echinopora as one of 8 chalice-umbrella genera. Minor disagreement on calcium upper bound (460 vs 450) resolved by averaging.

additional.name: Corals of the World – Echinopora lamellosa
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (genus Echinopora Lamarck 1816), colony growth form description (thin laminae in whorls/tiers), wild colony size documentation (stands exceeding 5 m across), and habitat note (dominant in shallow flat-substrate habitats). Not used for husbandry parameters.

additional.name: Reef Builders – ORA Sprung's Stunner Echinopora lamellosa
additional.notes: Used for ORA aquaculture history and trade name documentation (Sprung's Stunner — purple base, green polyps). Confirmed hardy LPS character, fast growth, sweeper tentacle caution at night.
